architecture.summary: Knowledge Rumination augments pre-trained language models by prepending a prompt ("As far as I know") to activate and consolidate latent knowledge already encoded in the model's parameters. The system operates in-context without external retrieval: the prompt triggers the model to surface and reuse internal knowledge representations, which are then incorporated into the final task prediction.

biblio.title: Knowledge Rumination for Pre-trained Language Models

claims.statement: Pre-trained language models may have already encoded rich knowledge in their pre-trained parameters but fail to fully utilize them when applying to knowledge-intensive tasks
By simply adding a prompt like 'As far as I know' to PLMs, the model can review related latent knowledge and inject them back into the model for knowledge consolidation
Knowledge Rumination is effective across various language models, including RoBERTa, DeBERTa, and GPT-3
The approach demonstrates effectiveness on six commonsense reasoning tasks and GLUE benchmarks
